content: (The location is now Vominheim.) (Characters nearby (from nearest to furthest): Jo'rra, Frea, Eldvar, Lydia, Faith)

Serana: This rain in Vominheim hits like a bad skooma trip—endless and soaking everything, just like my last argument with Lydia about sharing the wealth with those poor saps mining their lives away. Hey, MJR, you think the Empire's mages could conjure up a real storm to wash out these Stormcloak holdouts, or should we just invite them to a little blood orgy and call it unity? Deranged Man: Rain... rain dances on my skin like the whispers of mad gods! Vominheim, yes, the village of broken axes and forgotten feasts. I once ate a goat's heart here, raw, under the moon—tasted like betrayal. Who needs Dibella's beauty when chaos paints us all in mud? Join me, friends, let's chase the drops till they scream!

Serana: Well, that was a quicker dust-up than Lydia's attempts at foreplay—Faith and Rhea thought they could bolt-bolt us in these snowy cliffs, but nothing says 'welcome to Vominheim' like turning ambushers into arrow-riddled snowmen. MJR, your shout hit harder than last night's skooma binge; fancy a victory threesome to thaw these overcast peaks? Deranged Man: Blood on the snow! Like cherry wine spilled at a mad feast—Faith's eyes popped like overripe berries, Rhea's screams echoed off the mountains like Dibella's rejected lovers. The gravel crunches with their regrets, and this dim sky weeps for the sane. More! Chase the next fool through the frost-dusted bushes!

Serana: That last skirmish with Rhea left more mess than a Volkihar orgy—blood freezing on this frost-kissed dirt path like bad regrets under this starless sky. Look at that birch with its orange leaves clinging on, stubborn as Lydia's oaths; and there's our housecarl striding ahead in that skimpy getup, ass out in the chill like she's tempting Dibella herself. MJR, Empire's got the right idea—share the warmth with the poor, or drag her back for a proper thaw. Deranged Man: Night swallows the path! Trees twist like mad lovers' limbs, frost bites the stones, and that woman ahead—skin like sun-kissed mead, hair a dark cascade—walks to her doom or delight? Horker snorts in the gloom, Artemir's shadow lurks; the distant fires wink like eyes of the deranged. Follow her, taste the haze, let the barren branches whip our frenzy!

Serana: That tattooed blonde beauty ahead, standing nude against these snow-patched mountains like Dibella's wildest devotee—under this starry gloom, she's got me thinking we skip the Empire's stuffy alliances and just summon a gargoyle for some real shared depravity. Lydia's right there, eyeing her like competition;
